D8 AnthropologyL3 ApplyReference

Azo Coupling — One Reaction, All the Colours

Azo bond Ar–N=N–Ar' between a diazonium and an aromatic gives 70% of all industrial dyes; substituent choice tunes wavelength from yellow to red to violet

The –N=N– azo group extends conjugation between two aryl rings. Electron-donating substituents on one side and electron-withdrawing on the other push absorption to longer wavelengths (orange → red → violet). Same reaction → infinite palette by molecular design.

Pick a coupler / electrophile pair; the swatch shows the dye colour.
